headline: A US government export-control order forcing Anthropic to pull Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the week.
body: Multiple channels report that the US government ordered Anthropic to withdraw Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 overnight, citing export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability, with the action extending to EU jurisdictions. Government regulation led the rotation alongside framing around frontier-AI public access and human control. In parallel, two diffusion-based text systems broke the 1,000 tokens per second mark: Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma on H100 GPUs and Xiaomi's TileRT-backed MiMo trillion-parameter MoE using FP4 quantization and speculative decoding. Model Context Protocol entered as a new technique, with IBM tying it to design systems for agentic workflows.
